questions.question_html: Find the LCM of 12 and 18.
questions.solution_html:
Clearly, we can see that common multiples of 12 and 18 are: 36, 72, 108 etc 
The lowest of these is 36.
Now, Let us see another method to find LCM of these two numbers.
The prime factorization of 12 and 18 are :
12 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3;
18 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
In these prime factorizations, the maximum number of times the prime factor 2 occurs is two; this happens for 12. Similarly, the maximum number of times the factor 3 occurs is two; this happens for 18. The LCM of the two numbers is the product of the prime factors counted the maximum number of times they occur in any of the numbers.
Thus, in this case: LCM = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 36.

questions.question_html: Find the LCM of 24 and 90.
questions.solution_html:
Clearly, The prime factorizations of 24 and 90 are:
24 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 
90 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
In the above prime factorizations the maximum number of times the prime factor 2 occurs is three for 24. Similarly, the maximum number of times the prime factor 3 occurs is two for 90.
The prime factor 5 occurs only once in 90.
Therefore, LCM of 24 and 90 = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 360

questions.question_html: Find the LCM of 40, 48 and 45.
questions.solution_html:
The prime factorizations of 40, 48 and 45 are given as follows:
40 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
48 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
45 = 3 × 3 × 5
The prime factor 2 appears maximum number of times which is four, in the prime factorisation of 48, the prime factor 3 occurs maximum number of two times in the prime factorisation of 45, The prime factor 5 appears one time in the prime factorisations of 40 and 45, we take it only once.
Therefore, we have required LCM = (2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2) {tex}\times{/tex} (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3) {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 720

questions.question_html: Two tankers contain 850 litres and 680 litres of kerosene oil respectively. Find the maximum capacity of a container which can measure the kerosene oil of both the tankers when used an exact number of times.
questions.solution_html:
The required container has to measure both the tankers in a way that the count is an exact number of times.
So, its capacity must be an exact divisor of the capacities of both the tankers.
Moreover, this capacity should be maximum. Thus, the maximum capacity of such a container will be the HCF of 850 and 680.
We can find it as follows :
 
Therefore, the factorised form of the numbers is:
850 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 and
680 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2
The common factors of the given numbers 850 and 680 are 2, 5 and 17.
And thus, the HCF of 850 and 680 is 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 170.
Hence, the maximum capacity of the required container is 170 litres.
It will fill the first container in 5 refills and the second in 4 refills.

questions.question_html: In a morning walk, three persons step off together. Their steps measure 80 cm, 85 cm and 90 cm respectively. What is the minimum distance each should walk so that all can cover the same distance in complete steps?
questions.solution_html:
Since, the distance covered by each one of the persons is required to be the same as well as minimum. So, for the required minimum distance, each should walk the lowest common multiple of the measures of their steps. Thus, we have to find find the LCM of 80, 85 and 90.
The LCM of 80, 85 and 90 is 12240.
The required minimum distance is 12240 cm.

Using divisibility tests, determine which of the following numbers are divisible by 2; by 3; by 4; by 5; by 6; by 8; by 9; by 10; by 11 (say, yes or no):

Number Divisible by
2 3 4 5 6 8 9 10 11
128

1586

406839
questions.solution_html:
Here, we have to divide the given set of numbers by the other set of given numbers to see whether each one of them is divisible or not.
To check this, we follow the divisibility rules as given below:

  1. For divisible by 2: Any number that has 0,2,4,6, or 8 in its one's place is divisible by 2.
  2. For divisible by 3: If the sum of all the digits of the number is multiple of 3, then it is divisible by 3.
  3. For divisible by 4: Any number whose last two digits (i.e. one's and ten's place digits) are divisible by 4 then, the number is divisible by 4.
  4. For divisible by 5: Any number that has 0 or 5 in its one's place is divisible by 5.
  5. For divisible by 6: If a number a divisible by both 2 and 3, then it is divisible by 6.
  6. For divisible by 8: Any number whose last three digits are divisible by 8 then, the number is divisible by 8.
  7. For divisible by 9: If the sum of all the digits of the number is multiple of 9, then it is divisible by 9.
  8. For divisible by 10: Any number that has 0 in its one's place is divisible by 10.
    So, after following these rules, we can see the results as are given below:

questions.question_html: The product of three consecutive numbers is always divisible by 6. Verify this statement with the help of some examples.
questions.solution_html:
Let the three consecutive numbers be n, (n + 1), ( n + 2). The product of these numbers be n(n + 1)(n + 2). We know that the product of three consecutive numbers is always divisible by 3. Out of the three consecutive numbers, one will be even. Which means the product is also divisible by 2.
Hence the product of three consecutive numbers is divisible by 3 and 2. Therefore the product of three consecutive numbers is also divisible by 6.
Ex.1: Take three consecutive number 21, 22 and 23.
21 is divisible by 3.
22 is divisible by 2.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 21 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 is divisible by 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 (= 6)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 21 {tex}\times{/tex} 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 23 is divisible by 6.
Ex.2: Take three consecutive numbers 47, 48 and 49.
48 is divisible by 2 and 3 both.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 48 is divisible by 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 6
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 47 {tex}\times{/tex} 48 {tex}\times{/tex} 49 is divisible by 6.

questions.question_html: Find the H.C.F of the numbers: 18, 60
questions.solution_html:
Factors of 18 are 1, 2, 3, 6, 9 and 18.
Factors of 60 are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10, 12, 15, 20, 30 and 60.
∴ Common factors of 18 and 60 are 1, 2, 3 and 6.
Highest of these common factors is 6.
∴ H.C.F. of 18 and 60 is 6.

∴ Common factors of 27 and 63 are 1, 3 and 9.
Highest of these common factors is 9.
∴ H.C.F. of 27 and 63 is 9.

questions.question_html: Find the H.C.F of the numbers: 34, 102.

So HCF of 34 and 102 is 34

questions.question_html: Renu purchases two bags of fertiliser of weights 75 kg and 69kg. Find the maximum value of weight which can measure the weight of the fertiliser exact number of times.
questions.solution_html:
Factors of 75 are 1, 3, 5, 15, 25 and 75.
Factors of 69 are 1, 3, 23 and 69.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Common factors of 75 and 69 are 1 and 3.
Highest of these common factors is 3.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} H.C.F. of 75 and 69 is 3.
Hence, the maximum capacity of weight which can measure the weight of the fertiliser exact number of times is 3kg.

questions.question_html: The length, breadth and height of a room are 825cm, 675 cm and 450cm, respectively. Find the longest tape which can measure the three dimensions of the room exactly.
questions.solution_html:
Factors of 825 are 1, 3, 5, 11, 15, 25, 33, 55, 75, 165, 275 and 825.
Factors of 675 are 1, 3, 5, 9, 15, 25, 27, 45, 75, 135, 225 and 675.
Factors of 450 are 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 9, 10, 15, 18, 25, 30, 45, 50, 75, 90, 150, 225 and 450.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Common factors of 825, 675 and 450 are 1, 3, 5, 15, 25 and 75.
Highest of these common factors is 75.
Hence, the length of the longest tape which can measure the three dimensions of the room exactly is 75cm.

questions.question_html: Determine the smallest 3-digit number which is exactly divisible by 6, 8 and 12.
questions.solution_html:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L.C.M. of 6, 8 and 12 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 24
Multiples of 24 are 24, 48, 72, 96, 120, 144, ...
Hence, the smallest 3-digit number which is exactly divisible by 6, 8 and 12 is 120.

questions.solution_html:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L.C.M. of 8, 10 and 12 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 120.
Multiple of 120 are:
120 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 120, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 240, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 360, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 480, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 600, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 720, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 840, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 960, 120 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 1080, ....
Hence, the largest 3-digit number exactly divisible by 8, 10 and 12 is 960.

questions.question_html: The traffic lights at three different road crossings change after every 48 seconds, 72 seconds and 108 seconds respectively. If they change simultaneously at 7 a.m., at what time will they change simultaneously again?
questions.solution_html:
{tex}\therefore{/tex} L.C.M. of 48, 72 and 108 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 432
432 seconds = 7 min 12 seconds.
Hence, they will change simultaneously again 7 min 12 seconds after 7 a.m.

questions.question_html: Three tankers contain 403 litres, 434 litres and 465 litres of diesel, respectively. Find the maximum capacity of a container that can measure the diesel of the three containers exact number of times.
questions.solution_html:
Factors of 403 are 1, 13, 31 and 403.
Factors of 434 are 1, 2, 7, 14, 31, 62, 217 and 434.
Factors of 465 are 1, 3, 5, 15, 31, 93, 155 and 465.
Common factors of 403, 434 and 465 are 1 and 31.
Highest of these common factors is 31.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} H.C.F of 403, 434 and 465 is 31.
Hence, the maximum capacity of the container that can measure the diesel of the three containers exact number of times is 31 litres.

questions.question_html: Find the LCM of the numbers: 9 and 4
questions.solution_html:
LCM - Lowest common multiple
The LCM of two numbers is the smallest number that is a multiple of both the numbers, and  can be obtained as follows:

LCM = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 36
